description: Chronic condition marked by high blood sugar levels resulting from an inability of the body to produce sufficient insulin (Type 1) or use insulin effectively (Type 2).Symptoms can include excessive thirst, urination, hunger, unexplained weight loss, fatigue, blurred vision, slow-healing sores.

Chronic inflammatory lung disease characterized by recurrent episodes of wheezing, chest tightness, shortness of breath, and coughing, particularly at night or early morning.Symptoms can be triggered by allergens, irritants, exercise, or respiratory infections.

Enlargement of the thyroid gland located in the front of the neck.Can be caused by iodine deficiency, thyroid nodules, or Graves' disease (overactive thyroid).Symptoms can include visible swelling in the neck, difficulty swallowing, hoarse voice, and tightness in the throat.
